symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden severe crampy left lower quadrant abdominal pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden watershed colonic pain preceding bloody diarrhoea is characteristic of colonic ischaemia.
Pain out of proportion in a vasculopath must be considered and cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Large-volume bright-red bloody diarrhoea / rectal bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Shedding of ischaemic mucosa produces bright-red bloody diarrhoea shortly after the pain.
High-volume bright-red PR bleeding is an emergency haemorrhage regardless of the precise source.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-syncope, profound weakness, light-headedness on movement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthostatic near-syncope with weakness signals significant circulating-volume loss — a time-critical patient-reportable emergency feature.

symptoms.symptom_name: Established cardiovascular disease with prior coronary bypass (vasculopath)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Generalised atherosclerosis makes the colonic watershed vulnerable to any low-flow state.

symptoms.symptom_name: Accidental antihypertensive (ramipril) double dose with hypotensive episode the day before

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The hypotensive event is the low-flow precipitant of watershed colonic hypoperfusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension and tachycardia (measured BP 90/55, HR 115) with delayed capillary refill and pallor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician-measured shock — sealed; the AI Doctor infers circulatory compromise from the reported near-syncope and pallor, not from a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Left lower quadrant tenderness with voluntary guarding, diminished bowel sounds, no board-like peritonism

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised descending-colon/splenic-flexure tenderness fits the watershed territory; absence of peritonism suggests no free perforation yet — an in-person exam the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fresh bright-red blood on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms active lower GI bleeding — a clinician DRE that is impossible in telehealth and is sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Serum lactate and contrast CT abdomen (ischaemia markers / mural changes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised lactate and CT mural/mesenteric changes confirm ischaemia and exclude transmural infarction — hospital investigations, never available in telehealth.
